title: Test native CatBoost with ordered categorical statistics instead of manual frequency encodings
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality categoricals (`Driver`, `Race`, their combinations) and CatBoost’s ordered target statistics were built for exactly this setting.
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality categoricals (`Driver`, `Race`, their combinations) and CatBoost’s ordered target statistics were built for exactly this setting. The current solutions already rely heavily on manual count/frequency features; CatBoost can learn richer leakage-resistant categorical interactions directly.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoost on raw categoricals plus the strongest numeric features, with rows kept in chronological order and `has_time=True` so permutations do not destroy temporal order. Compare against LightGBM/XGBoost using the same purged grouped folds; then blend if the errors are complementary.
expected_effect: Can capture driver/race/compound interaction structure that hand-built counts miss, especially for sparse drivers appearing in only some seasons.
risk: If row order is not meaningful within folds, `has_time` can hurt; also avoid one-hot preprocessing that prevents CatBoost from building CTR-style features.
